title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
description: Without knowing how the system handles user-supplied identifiers (e.g., database keys, file names), there's a risk of IDOR vulnerabilities. An attacker could manipulate these identifiers to access resources or perform actions that they shouldn't be authorized to do, potentially gaining elevated privileges.
attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in how the system resolves object references – manipulating URLs or parameters to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement access controls based on user roles and permissions.
Avoid using predictable or easily guessable object identifiers.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) to obfuscate the underlying resource.
